title: Compression Enables Generalization: Wake-Sleep Cycles for Logic Programming with LLM Integration
description: Knowledge bases in logic programming grow through fact accumulation but do not learn. We present DreamLog, a system that compresses its knowledge base through wake-sleep cycles, discovering rules that generalize to unseen entities. On a synthetic crafting domain with invented terms unknown to the LLM, compression enables 64% recall on unseen entities (up from 0%), with symbolic compression alone achieving 53%. A raw LLM baseline achieves 0%, confirming that the compression pipeline is genuinely necessary. On a canonical family tree, the full pipeline achieves 80% recall with 100% precision. A holdout sweep shows 100% recovery of held-out facts at up to 20% holdout in open-world verification mode, with the data density threshold appearing around 30%. These results are consistent with the Solomonoff induction thesis: shorter programs generalize better.
notes: Code and experiment registry available at https://github.com/queelius/dreamlog. All experiment scripts (EX23-EX25c) with full provenance and reproducible results.
